compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can dispatch the doc-writer agent and receive structured documentation output
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Create the dso:doc-writer dedicated agent with a full decision engine, 4-tier documentation schema enforcement, breakout heuristic, and safety guards.
**Why**: This is the walking skeleton for the entire epic — the core agent that all other stories depend on. It implements the documentation optimization logic from doc-writer-agent.md as a DSO named agent.
**Scope**:
- IN: Agent prompt file at plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md; decision engine gates (No-Op, User Impact, Architectural, Constraint) evaluated in order; 4-tier schema enforcement; breakout heuristic for Tier 3; CLAUDE.md read-only guard; diff truncation warning; structured no-op report output
- OUT: Sprint dispatch integration (Story b1c8-5b5e); standalone skill (Story f9e4-4b68); test fixtures (Story cdfb-28b3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the dso:doc-writer agent exists at plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md and can be dispatched via subagent_type: "dso:doc-writer"
  ← Satisfies: "A dedicated dso:doc-writer agent exists in plugins/dso/agents/"
- When this story is complete, the agent evaluates decision engine gates in order (No-Op → User Impact → Architectural → Constraint) and emits structured output per gate
  ← Satisfies: "The agent evaluates the decision engine gates in the specified order"
- When this story is complete, the agent emits a structured no-op report (reason string, list of gates evaluated, pass/fail per gate) when no doc changes are warranted
  ← Satisfies: "emits a structured no-op report"
- When this story is complete, the agent reads both epic context (story/task descriptions + success criteria) and git diff as input to the decision engine
  ← Satisfies: "The agent reads both epic context and git diff"
- When this story is complete, the agent enforces the 4-tier documentation schema: Navigation (root), User-Facing (/docs/user/), Living Reference (/docs/reference/), ADRs (/docs/adr/)
  ← Satisfies: "The agent enforces the 4-tier documentation schema"
- When this story is complete, the agent extracts Tier 3 sections exceeding ~1500 tokens or 3rd-level nesting into /docs/reference/subsystems/ with a summary link in the parent
  ← Satisfies: "The agent applies the breakout heuristic"
- When this story is complete, the agent treats CLAUDE.md as read-only and emits a suggested-change report rather than writing directly
  ← Satisfies: "The agent treats Tier 1 navigation files that are also safeguard files as read-only"
- When this story is complete, the agent logs a truncation warning when git diff exceeds context capacity and flags affected outputs as potentially incomplete
  ← Satisfies: "the agent logs a truncation warning"
- When this story is complete, the dso:doc-writer agent is listed in the CLAUDE.md Named-agent dispatch table with its model tier and dispatching skill
  ← Satisfies: "A dedicated dso:doc-writer agent exists" (adversarial review: consumer_impact)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Git diff may exceed agent context capacity — truncation warning logic must be robust and testable
- [Reliability] Breakout heuristic token estimation is approximate — consider using line count or header depth as more deterministic proxies
- [Maintainability] Title pattern matching ("Update project docs to reflect [epic summary]") couples sprint dispatch to preplanning's title generation — document this coupling
- [Architecture] Existing named agents are dispatched via subagent_type in caller SKILL.md files, NOT via agent-routing.conf (which is for external plugin routing). The doc-writer agent follows this same pattern — dispatch via subagent_type, no agent-routing.conf entry needed.

## Source Prompt Spec
doc-writer-agent.md (repo root) is the authoritative source. The agent prompt must faithfully incorporate its full detail: tier-specific writing styles, Tier 3 file names (system-landscape.md, domain-logic.md, operational-specs.md, known-issues.md), YAML frontmatter last_synced_commit requirement, breakout orchestrator notification format, and dual-audience style enforcement (humans vs LLM agents).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
